title: Ghostling
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Ghostling_child.jpg]] |name=Ghostling |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]{{CSWECite|I|338|Ghostling}} |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Tan]]''[[The Crystal Star]]'' |haircolor=Greenish-gold, silver, black''[[Episode I Adventures 5: The Ghostling Children]]'' |feathers= |eyecolor=Blue, violet, green,''[[Episode I Adventures 7: Capture Arawynne]]'' black |distinctions=Ethereal beauty, digitigrade posture, frail body |lifespan= |origin=[[Datar/Legends|Datar]] |habitat= |diet= |language=[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] }} {{Quote|Sleep well, my children, tonight.
''"''Though the forest is filled with light,
''"''Close your sweet eyes,
''"''While the sun flies
''"''Over the mountains. Good night.|A Ghostling lullaby|}} '''Ghostlings''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of ethereal [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Datar/Legends|Datar]]. While mostly [[Human/Legends|Human]]-like in anatomy, Ghostlings had key differences from that species. The Ghostlings' shiny, tan-[[Color/Legends|colored]] skin radiated an aura of light. Their lower [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] ended in five-toed feet that gave the species a tip-toe posture. Because they were accustomed to the low [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Ghostlings were quickly overexerted on planets with higher gravitational pull. They were easily injured in such conditions, with a mere touch enough to cause a bruise and with even a slight blow potentially proving [[Death/Legends|fatal]]. They lived in [[City/Legends|villages]] made up of nests, which hung from [[creeper vine]]s in Datar's forests of [[bayah]] [[Tree/Legends|trees]]. There the Ghostlings defended themselves from intruders with simple weapons and traps. Nevertheless, they were often troubled by [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]], especially during the latter [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In that [[Time/Legends|time]] period, Ghostling [[Youngling/Legends|children]] were sought after as pets or slaves by individuals who valued the Ghostlings for their extreme beauty and rarity. Indeed, in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], [[Gardulla Besadii the Elder/Legends|Gardulla]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] bought a cargo of Ghostling children from the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]] so that she could place them in her personal [[Gardulla's pleasure garden|pleasure garden]]. The children, among whom was [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Arawynne]], were freed by the Human slave boy [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and his friends. Ghostlings and Humans found each other mutually fascinating and attractive. However, due to the fragile structure of the Ghostlings, [[Reproduction/Legends|sexual relations]] between the two species inevitably proved fatal to the Ghostling partner. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|Ghostlings are very frail. It was kind of you to try to help. But now, how are you going to save yourselves?|[[Jira/Legends|Jira]], to Anakin Skywalker and company|''[[Episode I Adventures 6: The Hunt for Anakin Skywalker]]''}} Ghostlings were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] who largely resembled [[Human/Legends|Humans]] but for their [[faun]]-like characteristics and otherworldly beauty. Their bodies featured two [[Limb/Legends|arms]], two legs, and a head.''[[Episode I Adventures 8: Trouble on Tatooine]]'', character cards Iridescent, tan-[[Color/Legends|colored]] skin covered the thin and delicate frame and emitted a soft glow. Their wiry bodies were adapted to the light [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Datar/Legends|Datar]]. Each limb terminated in five digits; their fine fingertips were topped by gold-colored fingernails, and their feet were designed such that they always walked digitigrade with a quick and nimble gait.''[[Episode I Adventures 6: The Hunt for Anakin Skywalker]]'' Ghostlings were less surefooted in heavier-gravity environments, which they found cumbersome and tiring. Running was especially difficult and caused a Ghostling to be winded quickly. Ghostling [[Youngling/Legends|children]] were especially susceptible to the ill-effects of higher gravity, which made respiration difficult and fatigued them unless they lay prone. The Ghostling head featured a [[Nose/Legends|nose]], [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]], and two sparkling, wide [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], which came in shades of [[Color/Legends|blue]], violet, green, and black. Their [[Ear/Legends|ears]] lay flat against the [[Bone/Legends|skull]], narrowing to pointed tips. The species' [[Hair/Legends|hair]] grew long and fine from the top of the head, curling in some specimens, and surrounding the head like an aura. Ghostlings sported locks of greenish-gold, silver, or black. Their eyesight and hearing were keen, although their intelligence was otherwise not exceptional. The Ghostling species was divided into both male and female [[Gender/Legends|genders]], who [[Reproduction/Legends|reproduced]] by sexual intercourse. Their young were still considered juveniles at eight [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]]. Members of the species seemed to radiate a faultless beauty that species as diverse as [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]],''[[Episode I Adventures 8: Trouble on Tatooine]]'' [[Dug/Legends|Dugs]], and [[Sakiyan/Legends|Sakiyans]] found inspiring. Fear was one of the few things that could mar a Ghostling's beauty by flushing the face of color. Humans especially found Ghostlings sexually attractive. However, members of the low-gravity species were extremely fragile. Merely touching a Ghostling could leave a purple bruise, and striking one with a light blow might break a bone. Thus, sexual intercourse with a Human was certain to cause the Ghostling partner's [[Death/Legends|death]]. Members of the species required food and [[Water/Legends|water]] to live, and lack of nourishment caused the [[Stomach/Legends|stomach]] to rumble. Ghostlings were diurnal creatures, [[Sleep/Legends|sleeping]] at night. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|No, I'm not like that, not one of them. I used to… I used to plague humans. I was fascinated with your kind. I followed, I teased…you are so exciting!…and I thought, it might be worth it just to partake of a human, even as the last experience of my life.|A Ghostling, to Han Solo|}} The Ghostlings were native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Datar, where they lived in [[City/Legends|villages]] made up of several nests. The nests were immense bowl-shaped structures woven from [[creeper vine]]s that hung from the native [[bayah]] [[Tree/Legends|trees]].''[[Episode I Adventures 8: Trouble on Tatooine]]'' A mouth on each such dwelling afforded its inhabitants entry and egress. While some Ghostlings placed their nests about two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] from the ground, others chose to nest high in the trees. Ghostlings were expert weavers, able to fashion objects not only from vines but also from reeds. Their arboreal homes made them expert climbers. Ghostling [[Parent/Legends|parents]] inhabited the same nest as their children. The young learned the location of their nest well enough to find it easily even after nightfall. Ghostling mothers sang [[Music/Legends|lullabies]] to put their children to sleep. While members of the species were not known for their martial prowess, they defended their settlements with simple weapons and traps. For instance, Ghostlings knew how to place trip wires to keep away intruders, and how to lay pit traps lined with pointed sticks. Ghostling [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]] fired small [[Bow/Legends|bows]] and [[Arrow/Legends|arrows]], hurled native fauna known as [[Blaze bug/Legends|blaze bugs]], or fired small [[Stun blaster/Legends|stun blasters]] at enemies. Blaze bugs were also repurposed as natural light sources. Ghostlings were capable of speaking [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. They otherwise communicated through gestures and involuntary actions, such as crying when distraught. Ghostling toddlers were prone to suck their fingers. Names found among the species included the female name Arawynne, the male names Borofir, Conno, and Coniel, as well as the name Alamar. [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] was a recognized rank in Ghostling society; such individuals were considered leaders among their people. Appropriate garb for a Ghostling princess included an elaborate dress, a shawl, and a belt, all embellished with [[Gemstone/Legends|jewels]]. Despite the two species' sexual incompatibility, Ghostlings found Humans mesmerizing. Their natural beauty made Ghostlings experts at seduction as well. They knew of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and respected the members of its [[Jedi Order/Legends|Order]]. ==History== {{Quote|Do you have a name, pretty one?''"''
''"''Arawynne. Princess Arawynne.''"''
''"''Princess no more…Now, ''slave'' Arawynne.|Sebulba kidnaps Arawynne from Datar|}} [[File:Djas_puhr.jpg|thumb|left|The Sakiyan bounty hunter Djas Puhr raided Datar to kidnap Ghostling children for his employer, the Dug Podracer Sebulba.]] The Ghostlings evolved on Datar,{{CSWECite|I|173|Datar}} a low-gravity world in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' where the forests encouraged them to develop into expert climbers with keen eyes and ears, and a tip-toe posture. The planet became part of the known [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] at some point between [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000]] to [[8000 BBY]]. By [[1004 BBY]], the Ghostling homeworld fell within the confines of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and Datar was allied with that government by [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. The Ghostlings had become highly desired commodities in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] trade, however, so Ghostling settlements on their homeworld were susceptible to raids by outsiders. In 32 BBY, for example, the Sakiyan [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Djas Puhr/Legends|Djas Puhr]] and his [[Abyssin/Legends|Abyssin]] partner, [[Gondry]], on orders from their employer, the Dug [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]], invaded a [[Unidentified Ghostling village|Ghostling village]], evaded the inhabitants' attempts to fight them off, and kidnapped a group of Ghostling children. Among those they abducted was the seven-year-old Princess [[Arawynne]]. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' the Ghostlings sided with the Republic. After the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the eruption of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operated on Datar. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], however, Imperial forces [[Attack at Datar|conquered]] the Rebels in a Pyrrhic victory.{{StoryCite|story=A Hunter's Fate: Greedo's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}} The species found themselves in [[Imperial Space]] in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], and their homeworld formed part of the Imperial [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] core territories during his [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. Circa [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the Ghostling home [[Datar system/Legends|system]] had a population of one to ten million. By [[137 ABY]], their planet was within the bounds of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Sith Empire]]. ==Ghostlings in the galaxy== {{Quote|Please, can you help us?''"''
''"''Help? I think not. You're worth a lot of money to my master. Ghostlings are hard to find.''"''
''"''I didn't do anything bad! I want to go home.''"''
''"''We didn't take you because you're bad. We took you because you are what you are.|Two Ghostling children and Dug slaver [[Khiss (Dug)|Khiss]]|}} [[File:Warucloseup.jpg|thumb|right|upright|In 14 ABY, a Ghostling female became a member of a cult that worshiped the extra-dimensional being Waru.]] By the latter years of the Galactic Republic, Ghostlings were highly valuable as slaves and pets due to their exotic looks and rarity. However, they were hard for slavers to come by, which made them expensive when they came to market. For instance, a typical Ghostling sold for 30,000 [[Wupiupi/Legends|wupiupi]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]]—still less than rarer species, such as [[Columi/Legends|Columi]], but three times the going rate for a Bothan. After kidnapping the Ghostling Princess Arawynne and other Ghostling children, Djas Puhr and Gondry brought their captives to the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where their boss, Sebulba, was preparing for the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] of [[Boonta Eve Classic (32 BBY)/Legends|32 BBY]].{{DB|character|kitster|Banai, Kitster}} The crate holding the Ghostlings came to the attention of the enslaved Human children [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], [[Kitster Chanchani Banai|Kitster Banai]], and [[Amee/Legends|Amee]], as well as their [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] companion [[W. Wald]] and [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] friend [[Pala Kwi'teksa]]. Skywalker vowed to free the children before Sebulba could implant [[Slave tracking device|slave transmitters]] within the Ghostlings, devices that exploded should a slave run away. Meanwhile, Arawynne, eldest of the group of captured Ghostlings at seven years of age, assumed a leadership position and consoled her younger companions, who included [[Alamar]], three-year-old [[Borofir]],{{CSWECite|I|87|Borofir}} five-year-old [[Conno]],{{CSWECite|I|138|Conno}} and three-year-old [[Coniel]]. Sebulba sold the Ghostlings to [[Gardulla Besadii the Elder/Legends|Gardulla]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], who wanted them to act as living lawn ornaments—and prey for roving [[Diet/Legends|carnivores]]—in the underground [[Gardulla's pleasure garden|pleasure garden]] of her [[Imperial Station Bravo|palace]] on the outskirts of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]]. Skywalker and his companions, now joined by the Bothan [[Dorn (Bothan)|Dorn]], infiltrated the compound and freed the captives, using the unimplanted slave trackers as bombs to blast their way out of the palace on Arawynne's suggestion. Tatooine's standard gravity impeded their escape, however, so Banai led them into the pleasure garden to hide until he and his friends could bring help. Arawynne hid the children in the garden's forests, which were similar to those of Datar. There she befriended the [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] gardeners and the [[Wistie/Legends|Wisties]] Gardulla had [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] there. The Ghostlings learned how to avoid the dangers of the pleasure garden as well as how to turn the hostile terrain to their advantage. They wove themselves a hideaway from reeds and set up simple traps to ward off intruders. When Skywalker and his accomplices returned to mount a rescue, the smaller Ghostling children sneaked into the fan control room and turned off the exhaust ports to allow the group to escape through the pleasure garden's ventilation system. With Wisties acting as lookouts, Arawynne confronted Djas Puhr in a forested area, leading him into a pit trap with the [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] help of a [[Unidentified Wistie captive|Wistie companion]]. Nevertheless, at the last moment, Sebulba appeared in the desert outside the group's intended exit vent and forced the Ghostlings to retreat into Djas's clutches. An enraged Gardulla scheduled the group for execution in the [[Mos Espa Grand Arena/Legends|Mos Espa Grand Arena]]. However, Skywalker escaped, sneaked into the arena's holding cells, freed the children, and led them outside. Using a stolen [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]], Skywalker flew the freed Ghostlings to a formation called [[Bantha Rock]], and, with money he and his friends had collected, he paid the Human [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Rakir Banai]] to transport the Ghostlings away from Tatooine. In [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]], a female [[Unidentified Ghostling Waru devotee|Ghostling]] gave up a life of seducing Humans to become a devotee of the extra-dimensional being [[Waru]]. She confronted the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] smuggler [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] at [[Crseih Station]] to preach about her faith, although Solo dismissed her with the assumption that she was soliciting him for sex—which he knew would kill her. The Ghostling admitted his assumption would have been correct in the past, but that Waru had shown her a new way of life. ==Behind the scenes== Author [[Vonda N. McIntyre]] introduced the Ghostling species in her [[1994]] novel, ''[[The Crystal Star]]'', which established the species' basic physical appearance, extreme beauty, and sexual attraction to Humans. Both [[Stephen J. Sansweet]]'s ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' and [[Bill Slavicsek]]'s ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'', published in [[1998]] and [[2000]], respectively, include entries for the species that draws from McIntyre's novel.''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' Author [[Dave Wolverton]] largely ignored the seductive aspects of the species and instead emphasized the Ghostlings' ethereal beauty in his young-adult novels and game books published in [[2000]] as part of the [[Star Wars: Episode I Adventures|''Episode I Adventures'']] series. ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]], incorporates material from both McIntyre's and Wolverton's works. In one entry, that for the Ghostling Borofir, the book claims that Gardulla the Hutt intended to sell her Ghostling captives on the Mos Espa slave market. As this differs significantly from the plot of the young-adult novels, the encyclopedia seems to be in error. The [[Episode I Adventures 8: Trouble on Tatooine|game book]] for ''[[Episode I Adventures 8: Trouble on Tatooine]]'' allows readers to take on the role of several different characters, including the Ghostling Princess Arawynne, as that character crawls through the sand tunnels under the Mos Espa Grand Arena to free the Ghostling children before they are executed.''[[Episode I Adventures 5: The Ghostling Children]]'' However, the novel version of the story attributes this deed to Anakin Skywalker, as does [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 12|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 12]],{{FactFile|12|ANA 1-4|Anakin Skywalker}} thus indicating that only the selection of Skywalker reflects the official events of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity. ''Trouble on Tatooine'' also includes a card that features an illustration of Arawynne, the first graphical depiction of the species. ==Appearances== *''[[Episode I Adventures 5: The Ghostling Children]]'' *''[[Episode I Adventures 6: The Hunt for Anakin Skywalker]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Episode I Adventures 7: Capture Arawynne]]'' *''[[Episode I Adventures 8: Trouble on Tatooine]]'' *''[[The Crystal Star]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{FactFile|12|ANA 1-4|Anakin Skywalker}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|kitster|Banai, Kitster}} *{{DB|character|gardullathehutt|Gardulla the Hutt}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Inner Rim sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Ghostlings]] }}
